purpose: Provide reusable examples showing how UI Architect should protect image prompt or rendered UI outputs without creating project-specific rules.

layout_preservation: Preserve header, orientation panel, main product panel and support strip zones.
Do not add landing-page hero sections, dashboard widgets or catalog grids.
hierarchy_preservation: Main product panel remains rank 1 visual focus.
Primary CTA is visible but does not overpower the main product content.
legibility_preservation: All visible UI text must be readable at mockup scale.
Do not render random glyphs or pseudo-text in buttons, labels or cards.
Keep body copy short enough to avoid dense blocks.
state_preservation: Active step must be visually distinct from secondary steps.
Inactive or secondary items must remain lower priority.
composition_constraints: Do not let decorative elements compete with the primary action.
Keep alignment consistent across repeated components.
artifact_constraints: No fake charts or metrics not specified by the component tree.
acceptance_criteria: Accepted if layout, hierarchy, legibility and state mapping are visually preserved.
Rejected if the render invents new sections, unreadable text, fake metrics or changes the primary flow.

layout_preservation: Preserve top context, main task card, support list and bottom action zones.
Do not add desktop navigation or multi-column layout.
hierarchy_preservation: Main task card remains the first visual priority.
Sticky bottom CTA remains clear and reachable.
Support items remain secondary and limited.
legibility_preservation: Card title and helper text must not collapse into unreadable text.
state_preservation: Support items remain informational, not competing actions.
composition_constraints: Use vertical rhythm and safe-area spacing.
Avoid identical card stacking as the dominant layout.
artifact_constraints: No added charts, metrics or testimonial cards.
acceptance_criteria: Accepted if the mobile screen is task-focused and readable.
Rejected if it becomes a landing page, dashboard or unreadable card stack.

layout_preservation: Preserve form title, helper context, input group, validation area and primary action.
Do not add unrelated marketing blocks.
hierarchy_preservation: Form title and current required input remain primary.
Validation help is visible but secondary.
Primary action is clear and singular.
legibility_preservation: Error or helper text must be legible and near the relevant input.
Do not rely on color alone to communicate validation.
state_preservation: Inputs must show default, focus, disabled or error states when relevant.
Primary action state must match form validity.
composition_constraints: Use a clear vertical form rhythm.
Avoid placing multiple primary actions near the form.
Keep help text close to the field it explains.
artifact_constraints: No fake form data unless specified.
acceptance_criteria: Accepted if the form is legible, linear and state-aware.
Rejected if validation states are unclear, labels are unreadable or the render invents unrelated sections.
